claim: For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 is prime.
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 is odd.
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 ≡ 1 (mod 2).
For all integers n >= 0, n(n+1) is even.
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 is not divisible by 2.
For all integers n >= 0, gcd(n, 41) divides n^2 + n + 41.
For all integers n >= 0, if 41 divides n then 41 divides n^2 + n + 41.
For all integers n >= 0 with n < 41, n^2 + n + 41 >= 41.
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 ≡ 0 (mod 5) is possible.
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 is not congruent to 0 modulo 3.
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n ≡ 2 (mod 3) implies n^2 + n + 41 ≢ 0 (mod 3).
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 > n for all n >= 0.
For all integers n >= 0, phi(n^2 + n + 41) = n^2 + n + 40 if n^2 + n + 41 is prime.
predicate: (n < 0) or ((not is_prime(n * n + n + 41)) or (phi(n * n + n + 41) == n * n + n + 40))

claim: For all integers n >= 0, tau(n^2 + n + 41) = 2 if n^2 + n + 41 is prime.
predicate: (n < 0) or ((not is_prime(n * n + n + 41)) or (tau(n * n + n + 41) == 2))

claim: For all integers n >= 0, sigma(n^2 + n + 41) = n^2 + n + 42 if n^2 + n + 41 is prime.
predicate: (n < 0) or ((not is_prime(n * n + n + 41)) or (sigma(n * n + n + 41) == n * n + n + 42))

claim: For all integers n >= 0, if p is a prime divisor of n^2 + n + 41, then p > n.
predicate: (n < 0) or (all(p > n for p in range(2, int(n*n+n+42)) if (n*n+n+41) % p == 0 and is_prime(p)))

claim: For all integers n >= 0 with n < 41, any prime divisor of n^2 + n + 41 is greater than n.
predicate: (n < 0) or (n >= 41) or (all(p > n for p in range(2, int(n*n+n+42)) if (n*n+n+41) % p == 0 and is_prime(p)))

claim: For all integers n >= 0, consecutive values of n^2 + n + 41 differ by 2n + 2.
For all integers n >= 0, gcd(n^2 + n + 41, (n+1)^2 + (n+1) + 41) divides 2n + 2.
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 is not divisible by small primes like 2, 3, 5.
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 is coprime to 6.
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 ≡ 41 (mod n) when n divides 41.
For all integers n >= 0, if n divides 41 then n^2 + n + 41 ≡ 41 (mod n).
For all integers n >= 0, the sequence n^2 + n + 41 contains infinitely many primes.
The density of primes in the sequence n^2 + n + 41 does not approach zero.
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 is not a perfect square.
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 > n^2, so it is strictly larger than the nearest perfect square below.
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 ≢ 0 (mod 7).
For all integers n >= 0, if n^2 + n + 41 ≢ 0 (mod 7) then it has no factor of 7.
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 is squarefree (not divisible by any perfect square > 1).
predicate: (n < 0) or (all(vp(n*n+n+41, p) <= 1 for p in range(2, int(pow(n*n+n+42, 0.5))+1) if is_prime(p)))

claim: For all integers n >= 0, if n^2 + n + 41 is squarefree and prime, then tau(n^2 + n + 41) = 2.
predicate: (n < 0) or ((not is_prime(n*n+n+41)) or (all(vp(n*n+n+41, p) <= 1 for p in range(2, int(pow(n*n+n+42, 0.5))+1) if is_prime(p))) or (tau(n*n+n+41) == 2))

claim: For all integers n >= 0, (n^2 + n + 41) + (n+1)^2 + (n+1) + 41 is even.
For all integers n >= 0, the sum of the first k values of the sequence is approximately (k^4)/4 + O(k^3).
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 ≡ n^2 + n (mod 41).
For all integers n >= 0, if n^2 + n + 41 is prime and greater than 41, then n^2 + n ≢ 0 (mod 41).
For all integers n >= 0, gcd(n^2 + n + 41, 41) is either 1 or 41.
For all integers n >= 0, if n ≡ 0 (mod 41) then n^2 + n + 41 ≡ 41 (mod 41).
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 ≢ 1 (mod 2).
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 is coprime to 30.
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 is coprime to both 6 and 5.
For all integers n >= 0, the multiplicative order of 2 modulo n^2 + n + 41 divides phi(n^2 + n + 41).
predicate: (n < 0) or ((n*n+n+41) == 1) or (all(pow(2, i, n*n+n+41) != 1 for i in range(1, phi(n*n+n+41))) or pow(2, phi(n*n+n+41), n*n+n+41) == 1)

claim: For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 has at most 2 distinct prime factors if it is composite.
predicate: (n < 0) or (is_prime(n*n+n+41) or (len([p for p in range(2, int(pow(n*n+n+41, 0.5))+1) if is_prime(p) and (n*n+n+41) % p == 0]) <= 2))

claim: For all integers n >= 0, if n^2 + n + 41 is squarefree and composite, it is a product of at most 2 primes.
predicate: (n < 0) or (not (all(vp(n*n+n+41, p) <= 1 for p in range(2, int(pow(n*n+n+42, 0.5))+1) if is_prime(p)) and not is_prime(n*n+n+41))) or (len([p for p in range(2, int(pow(n*n+n+41, 0.5))+1) if is_prime(p) and (n*n+n+41) % p == 0]) <= 2)

claim: For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 ≢ 0 (mod 11).
For all integers n >= 0, n^2 + n + 41 avoids the residue class 0 modulo 11.
For all integers n >= 0, the polynomial n^2 + n + 41 evaluated at n gives a value whose prime factors (if composite) are all greater than or equal to 41.
predicate: (n < 0) or (is_prime(n*n+n+41) or all(p >= 41 for p in range(41, int(pow(n*n+n+42, 0.5))+1) if is_prime(p) and (n*n+n+41) % p == 0))

claim: For all integers n >= 0, if n^2 + n + 41 is composite, all its prime factors exceed n.
predicate: (n < 0) or (is_prime(n*n+n+41) or all(p > n for p in range(2, int(pow(n*n+n+42, 0.5))+1) if is_prime(p) and (n*n+n+41) % p == 0))

claim: For all integers n >= 0, n(n+1)(n^2 + n + 41) is always even.
For all integers n >= 0, sigma(n^2 + n + 41) is even if n^2 + n + 41 is an odd prime.
